title: Choose a solicitation method - Request for standing offer | CanadaBuys

structure_blocks.heading: Buyer's Guide Choose a solicitation method - Request for standing offer
heading_path: Buyer's Guide Choose a solicitation method - Request for standing offer
structure_blocks.text: Request for standing offers A Request for standing offers (RFSO) is a method used to solicit offers for standing offer (SO) methods of supply. Standing offers (SOs) are pre-negotiated arrangements that streamline the procurement process for repeated supplies or services. RFSO s can be used to solicit offers through public advertisement on GETS , direct invitations using a source list where permitted or by inviting a single source only if conditions for a non-competitive process have been met. When creating an RFSO , you must include instructions on the use, purpose and limitations of the proposed SO . This involves incorporating instructions and conditions tailored for SO s. You must include the following key elements in an RFSO , as a minimum: a clear definition of the requirement and the period for making call-ups the anticipated number of SO s to be authorized for use instructions for offer preparation clear evaluation criteria and procedures, including basis of selection methodology for informing offerors about the evaluation outcomes of their offers a clear ranking methodology where applicable precise call-up procedures, including the work allocation method among multiple SO s a notification to offerors about the disclosure of their unit prices conditions specific to the RFSO and the SO resulting contract clauses applicable to ensuing call-ups the estimated utilization, whenever practical Standing offer procedures Call-up limits: A call-up against a SO constitutes an individual contract. Treasury Board contracting limits apply, and you, the buyer, will set the maximum call-up limit for identified users in the SO document, referencing Appendix A – Contracting Approvals of the Directive on the Management of Procurement as a guide. However, PSPC has the authority to further limit the value of individual call-ups. Financial limitation : You may include a limitation of expenditure in SO s. The standing offer authority will determine the need for including of a limit on the basis of the type of SO (Individual or Master), the level of control needed over total expenditures and client requirements. Identified users : The identified users authorized to make call-ups against SO could include any government department, agency or Crown corporation listed in Schedule I, Schedule I.1, Schedule II, Schedule III of the Financial Administration Act . Reporting requirements : The SO authority may indicate in the SO the reporting requirement for the offeror, and/or the client as applicable. In such case, the SO must indicate the time frame within which each report must be submitted following the reporting period.

raw_text:
Request for standing offers A Request for standing offers (RFSO) is a method used to solicit offers for standing offer (SO) methods of supply. Standing offers (SOs) are pre-negotiated arrangements that streamline the procurement process for repeated supplies or services. RFSO s can be used to solicit offers through public advertisement on GETS , direct invitations using a source list where permitted or by inviting a single source only if conditions for a non-competitive process have been met. When creating an RFSO , you must include instructions on the use, purpose and limitations of the proposed SO . This involves incorporating instructions and conditions tailored for SO s. You must include the following key elements in an RFSO , as a minimum: a clear definition of the requirement and the period for making call-ups the anticipated number of SO s to be authorized for use instructions for offer preparation clear evaluation criteria and procedures, including basis of selection methodology for informing offerors about the evaluation outcomes of their offers a clear ranking methodology where applicable precise call-up procedures, including the work allocation method among multiple SO s a notification to offerors about the disclosure of their unit prices conditions specific to the RFSO and the SO resulting contract clauses applicable to ensuing call-ups the estimated utilization, whenever practical Standing offer procedures Call-up limits: A call-up against a SO constitutes an individual contract. Treasury Board contracting limits apply, and you, the buyer, will set the maximum call-up limit for identified users in the SO document, referencing Appendix A – Contracting Approvals of the Directive on the Management of Procurement as a guide. However, PSPC has the authority to further limit the value of individual call-ups. Financial limitation : You may include a limitation of expenditure in SO s. The standing offer authority will determine the need for including of a limit on the basis of the type of SO (Individual or Master), the level of control needed over total expenditures and client requirements. Identified users : The identified users authorized to make call-ups against SO could include any government department, agency or Crown corporation listed in Schedule I, Schedule I.1, Schedule II, Schedule III of the Financial Administration Act . Reporting requirements : The SO authority may indicate in the SO the reporting requirement for the offeror, and/or the client as applicable. In such case, the SO must indicate the time frame within which each report must be submitted following the reporting period.

Request for standing offers

A Request for standing offers (RFSO) is a method used to solicit offers for standing offer (SO) methods of supply. Standing offers (SOs) are pre-negotiated arrangements that streamline the procurement process for repeated supplies or services. RFSO s can be used to solicit offers through public advertisement on GETS , direct invitations using a source list where permitted or by inviting a single source only if conditions for a non-competitive process have been met.

When creating an RFSO , you must include instructions on the use, purpose and limitations of the proposed SO . This involves incorporating instructions and conditions tailored for SO s.

You must include the following key elements in an RFSO , as a minimum:

a clear definition of the requirement and the period for making call-ups

the anticipated number of SO s to be authorized for use

instructions for offer preparation

clear evaluation criteria and procedures, including basis of selection

methodology for informing offerors about the evaluation outcomes of their offers

a clear ranking methodology where applicable

precise call-up procedures, including the work allocation method among multiple SO s

a notification to offerors about the disclosure of their unit prices

conditions specific to the RFSO and the SO

resulting contract clauses applicable to ensuing call-ups

the estimated utilization, whenever practical

Standing offer procedures

Call-up limits: A call-up against a SO constitutes an individual contract. Treasury Board contracting limits apply, and you, the buyer, will set the maximum call-up limit for identified users in the SO document, referencing Appendix A – Contracting Approvals of the Directive on the Management of Procurement as a guide. However, PSPC has the authority to further limit the value of individual call-ups.

Financial limitation : You may include a limitation of expenditure in SO s. The standing offer authority will determine the need for including of a limit on the basis of the type of SO (Individual or Master), the level of control needed over total expenditures and client requirements.

Identified users : The identified users authorized to make call-ups against SO could include any government department, agency or Crown corporation listed in Schedule I, Schedule I.1, Schedule II, Schedule III of the Financial Administration Act .

Reporting requirements : The SO authority may indicate in the SO the reporting requirement for the offeror, and/or the client as applicable. In such case, the SO must indicate the time frame within which each report must be submitted following the reporting period.
